rationale.completeness: The PR implements all major requirements: PluginProtocol interface with lifecycle hooks (on_startup, on_shutdown, before_request, after_request), OpenAPI schema extension capability, PluginRegistry, and app.add_plugin(). Tests cover lifecycle, request interception, OpenAPI, and composition. However, there's an inconsistency - openapi_schema is defined as a method in protocol.py but used as a @property in some tests and the contract document.
rationale.correctness: The core logic works but has issues: 1) The after_request hooks are called after super().__call__ completes but create a new PlainTextResponse instead of using the actual response, which could lose modifications. 2) If an exception occurs in route handling, after_request hooks may never execute since they're outside the try/except. 3) The routing.py integration adds _plugins to router but lifespan integration may not work correctly in all cases.
rationale.style: The code mostly follows FastAPI conventions with proper async/await, type hints, and docstrings. Minor style issues: inconsistent use of openapi_schema as property vs method, some test code has asyncio.run() calls inside test functions which could be simplified with pytest-asyncio.
rationale.quality: Well-structured with clear module separation (plugins/protocol.py, plugins/registry.py, plugins/utils.py). Good error isolation per plugin. Tests are comprehensive. The main quality concern is the response handling in after_request which creates a new response rather than properly capturing modifications made during the request.
